subj: thank you... free iPad update
OK, a few days ago I announced a contest to help
me come up with the tag line for my new blog...
and the prize was a free 64gb iPad.
I just wanted to give a quick update...
Basically, the response has been staggering... as
of right now I've had 1,704 people put in
suggestions!!
So first, I just want to say "THANK YOU" for such
an amazing response - reading through all the
entries has been an incredible gift.
I asked people to stick to a limit of 10
suggestions, but a few of them snuck in some
extras... so the total suggestions are now over
10,000 !!
That's a LOT of suggestions... but I've actually
gone through ALL of them, and we've been
narrowing
it down to our list of favorites.
I'm hoping to make the final selection within a
week or so.
If you still want to put in a suggestion, here's
the link:
http://www.sixinseven.com/winipad2.html
And again, THANK YOU ... I'm just blown away by
all these ideas.
best regards,
Jeff
P.S. On a more commercial note, the registration
for Expert's Academy is closing in just a few
hours... it closes for good TONIGHT.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 9 --

Subj: contest update... failure
OK, I just closed off the "free iPad" contest to
come up with the tag line for my new blog.
I had over 2,000 different entries (2,020 to be
exact)... and since most people were putting in up
to the max number of entries, the total tag number
of suggestions is somewhere around 20,000...
that's a LOT of tag lines!
And after going through all of them, I think I've
pretty much got my tag line... and I'll be
announcing the contest results when the blog goes
live in a few days.
This whole process of starting up a major new
project like this reminds me of something that I
heard in my mastermind meeting last week... it was
a quick liner that hit me like a bolt of
lightning. This is what it was:
"Give Yourself Permission To Fail"
Often, right when you're at the point of no
return... the point of going "live" with a
project... well, that's the point that you start
to get nervous.
All the last minute details start to loom, and
it's almost like the world is conspiring to stop
your project.
But, if you're honest with yourself, there's often
a big chuck of "fear of failure" thrown in.
As long as a project is not actually released...
well it can't fail can it? The dream is still
alive. Your project still has the POTENTIAL to
make a million dollars, it still has the POTENTIAL
to change the world...
Lots of people like to hang onto that POTENTIAL so
hard, and they're so scared of that potential not
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 9 --

coming through that they never release their
product, never open the doors of their business.
"Give Yourself Permission To Fail"
The wisdom hidden inside those five words is that
you only fail when you give up... any individual
project might go down in flames. But that's just
part of being an entrepreneur.
Remember that it's OK to lose battles, as long as
you win the war. And the only way to lose the war
is to give up.
The reality is that no entrepreneur is right every
time. There will always be projects, or products,
or even businesses that fail.
But if you are so scared of the INDIVIDUAL
failures that you never actually finish and
release your project... well that's a "big
picture" failure. That's how you lose the war.
"Give Yourself Permission To Fail"
The person who said that in my mastermind meeting
last week was Chris Knight... the founder and CEO
of EzineArticles.com.
Chris is one of the most inspirational people I
know... I've had the good fortune to know him for
a decade now - long before he started
EzineArticles. And I've gotten the chance to watch
him build that site from nothing right up to a
huge powerhouse that gets a million unique
visitors a DAY.
There is NO WAY you build a site that gets a
million visitors a day unless you have a lot of
small failures along the way.
It's OK to lose some battles. Just don't lose the
war.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
